goal: Preprocess the metabolomics expression matrix (2059 metabolites ×18 samples) for downstream statistical analysis, including missing value imputation, normalization, log transformation, and median scaling. The data originates from a murine CDI model with three experimental groups: high-iron diet before infection (FE, n=6), Clostridium difficile infection alone (CD, n=6), and healthy standard control (NC, n=6).

execution_steps.action: Read expression matrix. Load the CSV file with the first column (unnamed) as row names (molecule/metabolite IDs). Validate sample names match those in sampleinfo.csv. Extract18 sample columns corresponding to CD, FE, and NC groups (QC samples present in sampleinfo.csv but absent from the expression matrix, so they can be ignored).
execution_steps.goal: Load the raw expression data into a matrix format with molecules as rows and samples as columns, ready for preprocessing.

execution_steps.action: Missing value imputation. For each molecule (row), identify any missing (NA/NaN/empty) values. If missing values exist, compute the minimum positive value in that row and replace each missing value with half of that minimum. Note: Initial inspection shows0 NAs and0 zeros, so this step will be a no-op but ensures robustness for potential edge cases.
execution_steps.goal: Ensure all missing values are replaced with a small positive value (half of row minimum) to avoid issues in downstream log transformation and normalization.

execution_steps.action: Column sum normalization (relative expression). For each sample column, calculate the total sum of all metabolite intensities. Divide each value by its column sum to convert absolute intensities to relative abundance (proportions summing to1 per sample). This accounts for differences in total metabolite load between samples.
execution_steps.goal: Normalize the data to relative expression by scaling each sample to sum to1, removing systematic variation due to differences in total metabolite abundance across samples.

execution_steps.action: Log transformation. Check if any value in the normalized matrix exceeds100. Since the raw data has a maximum value of283.38 (>100), indicating non-log-transformed scale, apply log2 transformation: x = log2(x). If any zero values appear after normalization, add a small constant (e.g., the minimum positive value divided by2) before log transformation to avoid -Inf.
execution_steps.goal: Transform the data to log2 scale to approximate normal distribution, stabilize variance, and make low-abundance metabolite changes more comparable to high-abundance changes.

execution_steps.action: Median scaling per row (molecule). For each metabolite row, calculate the median value across all18 samples. Subtract this median from each value in the row (centering) and optionally divide by the median absolute deviation (scaling). The purpose is to center each metabolite's expression around zero, making patterns of up/down regulation comparable across metabolites regardless of their absolute abundance.
execution_steps.goal: Median-center each metabolite so that positive values indicate above-median expression and negative values indicate below-median expression, enabling cross-metabolite comparisons.

execution_steps.action: Post-processing validation and export. Validate the preprocessed matrix dimensions (2059 rows ×18 columns, row names preserved). Check for NaN or Inf values introduced during log transformation or scaling. Save the final preprocessed matrix as CSV in the tmp/ directory with molecule IDs as the first column and sample IDs as column headers.
execution_steps.goal: Ensure data integrity after preprocessing and export the clean, preprocessed expression matrix for downstream analysis.

notes: This is a metabolomics dataset (2059 annotated metabolites) from a murine CDI study. No user-specified preprocessing exceptions were identified in the research topic. The standard preprocessing pipeline applies: (1) missing value imputation (half-min per row) - no NAs detected but applied for robustness, (2) column sum normalization to relative abundance, (3) log2 transformation (required because max=283.38>100, indicating original non-log scale), (4) median scaling per row. Only2 out of2059 metabolites have values >100, but the workflow criterion (max value >100) triggers log transformation for the entire dataset. QC samples (Fc16YH_QC01-03) exist in sampleinfo.csv but are not present in the expression matrix columns, so they require no special handling. The molecule annotation table (metabolites.csv) can be used later for metabolite classification and pathway enrichment.
